---
title: "Intro to Statistics: Review"
crossref:
  lst-title: "🎯 Checkpoint 2."
  lst-labels: alpha a
format: live-html
sidebar: lesson-5
---

:::{.ltblue-container}
**Checkpoints on this Page:**

- [**Checkpoint 2.a:** Import Buoy Data](#lst-getcsv)
- [**Checkpoint 2.b:** Calculate Minimum and Maximum](#lst-minmaxlen)
:::
<hr>

```{pyodide}
#| include: false
import requests
import csv
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from io import StringIO

def load_class_data(csv_url):
    response = requests.get(csv_url)
    reader = csv.reader(StringIO(response.text))
    next(reader)  # Skip header
    rows = list(reader)
    
    knox_nitrate = [float(row[0]) for row in rows if row[0].strip()]
    greene_nitrate = [float(row[1]) for row in rows if len(row) > 1 and row[1].strip()]
    
    return knox_nitrate, greene_nitrate
```

## 💻 Import Buoy Data

:::{#lst-getcsv}

:::{.dkorange-container}
Import the buoy data from Cove Creek (Greene Co.) and Second Creek (Knoxville).

No code edits needed! Click the ▶️ Run Code button to run the block.
:::

```{pyodide}
#| persist: true
#| completion: false
csv_url = "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vSW_8RlRGj_e_WRurjN5hJr6KXhyK2DlujrV0xCLGCXxv698jTRBmpRO-aqHH-RaCvWep61tYE0_C7S/pub?gid=0&single=true&output=csv"

knox_nitrate, greene_nitrate = load_class_data(csv_url)

print("Knoxville (Second Creek) Nitrate values:", knox_nitrate)
print("Greene County (Cove Creek) Nitrite values:", greene_nitrate)
```

Import the buoy data!
:::

## 💻 Calculate Min, Max, & Length

:::{#lst-minmaxlen}

:::{.dkorange-container}
No code edits needed! 

Click the ▶️ Run Code button to run the block.
:::

### Knoxville Data (Second Creek)

```{pyodide}
#| persist: true
#| completion: false
min_knox = min(knox_nitrate)
max_knox = max(knox_nitrate)
len_knox = len(knox_nitrate)

print("Minimum of Knoxville nitrate:", min_knox)
print("Maximum of Knoxville nitrate:", max_knox)
print("Length of Knoxville nitrate:", len_knox)
```

### Greene County Data (Cove Creek)

```{pyodide}
#| persist: true
#| completion: false
min_greene = min(greene_nitrate)
max_greene = max(greene_nitrate)
len_greene = len(greene_nitrate)

print("Minimum of Greene nitrate:", min_greene)
print("Maximum of Greene nitrate:", max_greene)
print("Length of Greene nitrate:", len_greene)
```

Find the minimum, maximum, and length of the buoy data.
:::

## 💻 Calculate Measures of Center (Mean & Median)

:::{#lst-center}

:::{.dkorange-container}
No code edits needed! 

Click the ▶️ Run Code button to run the block.
:::

### Knoxville Data (Second Creek)

```{pyodide}
#| persist: true
#| completion: false
mean_knox = np.mean(knox_nitrate)
median_knox = np.median(knox_nitrate)

print("Mean of Knoxville nitrate:", mean_knox)
print("Median of Knoxville nitrate:", median_knox)
```

### Greene County Data (Cove Creek)

```{pyodide}
#| persist: true
#| completion: false
mean_greene = np.mean(greene_nitrate)
median_greene = np.median(greene_nitrate)

print("Mean of Greene nitrate:", mean_greene)
print("Median of Greene nitrate:", median_greene)
```

Find the mean and median of the buoy data.
:::

## 💻 Calculate Measures of Spread (Range)

:::{#lst-spread}

:::{.dkorange-container}
No code edits needed! 

Click the ▶️ Run Code button to run the block.
:::

### Knoxville Data (Second Creek)

```{pyodide}    
#| persist: true
#| completion: false    
knox_range = max(knox_nitrate) - min(knox_nitrate)

print("Range of Knoxville nitrate:", knox_range)
```

### Greene County Data (Cove Creek)

```{pyodide}
#| persist: true
#| completion: false
greene_range = max(greene_nitrate) - min(greene_nitrate)

print("Range of Greene nitrate:", greene_range)
```

Find the range of the buoy data.
:::

## 💻 Understanding Boxplots

Boxplots are a great way to visualize the spread of data. They show the min, max, and median of the data.

:::{#lst-knoxboxplot}

:::{.dkorange-container}
No code edits needed! 

Click the ▶️ Run Code button to run the block.
:::

```{pyodide}
#| completion: false
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
plt.boxplot(knox_nitrate, vert=True, patch_artist=True,
            whis=[0, 100],boxprops=dict(facecolor='plum'))
plt.ylabel('Nitrate (mg/L)', fontsize=12)
plt.xticks([])
plt.title('Boxplot of Knoxville Nitrate Values (Second Creek)', fontsize=13)
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis='y')
plt.show()
```

Create a boxplot for the Knoxville nitrate data.
:::
